source.title: Dropped Drill Pipe Incident - Thunder Horse PDQ Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 778

event.top_event: Loss of Containment of Suspended Load
actions_taken: Job was stopped and area secured
Injured Floor Hand reported to Medic and evacuated for medical evaluation
BSEE conducted on-site investigation on March 1, 2023
BSEE District Manager requested in-person meeting with BP and Valaris to discuss safety culture issues
Shut-in enforcement action issued under 30 CFR 250.107
event.summary: On February 18, 2023, at 21:27 hours, while pulling out of the hole with the 13th stand of 4-1/2 inch drill pipe on the Thunder Horse PDQ Platform, the Driller opened the BX4 elevator before the Pipe Racking System (PRS) could secure the stand. This caused the stand of drill pipe to fall across the rig floor towards the port side, landing on the auxiliary PRS hoist. Three Floor Hands in the yellow zone evacuated the area, with one slipping on the deck and injuring his knee and hip. The Floor Hand was evacuated the next day for medical evaluation. The incident could have resulted in multiple fatalities. Investigation revealed the Driller had a similar incident on January 3, 2023, and was not following Work Instructions WI-TH-DR-OPS-096.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District has no recommendations for the Office of Incident Investigations at this time
key_phrases: Pipe Racking System not in position
could have resulted in multiple fatalities
poor communications between Driller and Assistant Driller

threats.name: Premature opening of elevators before PRS secures pipe
Inadequate communication between Driller and Assistant Driller

controls.name: Work Instructions for Tripping Operations - POOH (WI-TH-DR-OPS-096)
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to confirm PRS holding drill string before unlatching elevators
controls.lod_basis: Primary operational procedure to prevent premature elevator opening

supporting_text: the Driller was not following the Work Instructions for Tripping Operations Pull Out of Hole (POOH) No. WI-TH-DR-OPS-096
Failure to confirm the PRS is holding the drill string before unlatching the elevators will result in dropping stand and injuring personnel
the Driller functioned the elevators to the open position before the Pipe Racking System (PRS) could secure the stand

controls.name: Communication protocol between Driller and Assistant Driller
controls.barrier_role: Verification that PRS has secured pipe before elevator release
controls.lod_basis: Primary operational communication to coordinate critical activities

supporting_text: Poor communications between the Driller and the AD - the Driller did not confirm with the AD that the PRS had secured the stand of pipe before opening the elevators

controls.name: Pipe Racking System (PRS)
controls.barrier_role: Physical system to secure drill pipe stand before elevator release
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered system to hold suspended load

supporting_text: before the Assistant Driller (AD) could get the Pipe Racking System (PRS) back into position and grab the 13th stand of 4-1/2 inch drill pipe. The Driller functioned the elevators to the open position
allowed the stand in the rotary to fall across rig floor

controls.name: BX4 Elevator system
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical system to hold drill pipe during tripping operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary lifting equipment to maintain suspended load

supporting_text: Prior to the incident, one of the witnesses stated that the elevator was having trouble opening up
the witness was told that once they got out of the hole, an effort would be made to see what was causing the problem
The Driller functioned the elevators to the open position

controls.name: Slips to hold drill string weight
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical device to support drill string weight in rotary
controls.lod_basis: Primary support system for drill string during connections

supporting_text: the Driller set the weight of the string on the slips and lowered the BX4 elevator position to clear the shoulder of the tool joint

controls.name: Red Zone exclusion area on rig floor
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to keep personnel clear of dropped object hazard zones

supporting_text: Although the red zone was clear at the time of the event, the stand of pipe fell across the yellow zone of the rig floor within close proximity of the three Floor Hands
The video from the rig floor shows three Floor Hands running across the rig floor Red Zone nearly being crushed by the stand of drill pipe

controls.name: Personnel egress and evacuation response
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to avoid impact from falling object
controls.lod_basis: Last-moment action to prevent injury after barrier failure

supporting_text: After realizing that the stand of pipe was falling, the three Floor Hands began to evacuate the area with one Floor Hand slipping on the deck
three Floor Hands running across the rig floor Red Zone nearly being crushed by the stand of drill pipe and one rig Floor Hand (flagger) standing clear of the falling pipe

controls.name: Non-slip deck surface in egress routes
controls.barrier_role: Physical surface condition to enable safe evacuation

supporting_text: one Floor Hand slipping on the deck
The BSEE Investigation Team walked down the egress route in the Red Zone and found the slippery surface where the Injured Person fell

controls.name: Medical response and evacuation capability
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: The injured Floor Hand reported to the Medic, was sent in for further evaluation, and did not return to the facility

controls.name: Training and supervision for short service employees
controls.barrier_role: Competence development and oversight during critical operations
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to ensure competent execution of tasks

supporting_text: Witnesses also stated right before the incident, one of the experienced Floor Hands was showing two short service employees (SSE) how to operate the Iron Rough Neck

controls.name: Equipment defect reporting and repair system
controls.barrier_role: Identification and correction of equipment degradation
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to detect and correct equipment issues before failure

supporting_text: Prior to the incident, one of the witnesses stated that the elevator was having trouble opening up. Furthermore, the witness was told that once they got out of the hole, an effort would be made to see what was causing the problem

controls.name: Incident reporting and learning from near-misses
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control through learning from previous events

supporting_text: During the interviews, BSEE was informed the Driller had a similar incident on January 3, 2023. The incident involved the Driller inadvertently opening the elevator, resulting in a stand of drill pipe being released before the Floor Hands could unscrew the stand of pipe with the Iron Rough Neck
the NOD DM requested an in-person meeting with BP and Valaris in the NOD office to discuss the MC 778 Thunderhorse PDQ safety culture involving several incidents that could have been serious events

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
